summary: Section 2b.2 already establishes that the dome's 'relabeling' pattern adopts standard-physics observations and renames the cause without producing new numerical content. Two globe fingerprints sharpen this further. (1) The values being relabeled are not bare empirical observations — they are theory-internal predictions whose numerical existence requires globe geometry. The Schumann fundamental 7.83 Hz comes from solving the angular Laplacian on a sphere with R = 6,371 km; M2 = 12.4206 h is the half-period of the lunar day, itself a function of two sidereal periods (Earth 23.93 h, Moon 27.32 d) that only exist for a rotating sphere with a Moon at ~60 R_Earth; S2 = 12.000 h is twice the inverse of the solar diurnal angular speed on a rotating sphere. The dome inherits the validation chain that globe physics already passed. (2) The section's count statistic — that only N of M reviewed WINs derive their predicted value from dome parameters — overstates the dome's independent contribution: among the few that DO derive from dome parameters, those parameters are themselves globe constants in disguise (DISC_R = π·R_Earth, VA = (π/2)c, sun_alt and moon_alt fitted to globe-WGS84 data per find_curve.py). The 'derives from dome parameters' criterion is satisfied by globe formulas with renamed variables. Both fingerprints close the same defender escape — that 'agreement with standard physics is just empirical consistency' — by showing the dome is inheriting derivation chains, not just observations.

fingerprint_1.name: The values being 'relabeled' are theory-internal predictions, not bare observations — each one explicitly requires globe geometry in its derivation chain
fingerprint_1.kernel_of_truth: The section currently frames relabeling as renaming the causal mechanism for a 'phenomenon already predicted and explained by standard physics.' That framing is correct at the structural level: relabeling does not generate new observable content. A defender, however, has the escape: 'every theory must agree with the same observations — empirical consistency is not theory-dependence. The dome agreeing with 7.83 Hz is no more suspicious than Newtonian gravity agreeing with Kepler's laws.' This escape relies on treating 7.83 Hz, M2 = 12.42 h, S2 = 12.00 h, and H₀ as raw empirical numbers that any theory can adopt. The fingerprint reframes them as theoretical eigenvalues / harmonic constituents whose very numerical definition presupposes spherical geometry.
fingerprint_1.but_here_is_why_it_fails: Trace each relabeled value to its source. (a) 7.83 Hz is the n=1 Earth–ionosphere cavity eigenvalue: f_n = (c/(2π R_E))·√(n(n+1)) with cavity-height correction. Direct numerical check: c/(2π·6,371) × √2 = 47.46/40.03 × 1.4142 ≈ 10.59 Hz raw, corrected to ~7.8 Hz by ionospheric losses (h ≈ 90 km). The √(n(n+1)) factor is the angular-Laplacian eigenvalue on a sphere — it has no analog in flat-disc geometry. (b) M2 = 12.4206 h is half the mean lunar day. The lunar day = 1/(1/T_E − 1/T_M) where T_E = 23.93419 h (Earth sidereal rotation) and T_M = 27.32158 d (Moon sidereal orbit at 60 R_Earth). Direct check: 1/(1/23.93419 − 1/(27.32158·24)) = 24.8409 h, half = 12.4205 h. Both T_E and T_M are sphere-geometry quantities — Earth's sidereal rotation only has meaning relative to fixed stars in 3D space; the Moon's 27.32-day sidereal period requires Keplerian orbital mechanics around a sphere at 60 R_Earth. The dome's local moon at 2,534 km altitude cannot orbit (no central gravity body, no Keplerian dynamics) and cannot produce a 27.32-day sidereal period. (c) S2 = 12.000 h is 360°/(2 × 15.000°/h) where 15.000°/h is the solar diurnal angular speed of a sphere rotating once per solar day. The factor of 2 is the semidiurnal harmonic. (d) K1 = 23.93419 h (referenced in WIN-049) is exactly the Earth sidereal rotation period — the time for a sphere to rotate once relative to fixed stars. (e) Hubble H₀ ≈ 70 km/s/Mpc is derived from Type Ia SNe luminosity distances and CMB peak positions, both of which are integrated 3D cosmological observables solved in spherical FLRW geometry. None of (a)–(e) is a 'measurement constant' the dome can simply adopt; each is the output of a derivation that uses spherical-Earth or 3D-spacetime geometry as an input. Adopting the value imports the geometry along with it.

globe_value.schumann_7p83: f₁ = (c/(2π R_E))·√(n(n+1)) for n=1 = (c/(2π·6,371))·√2 ≈ 10.59 Hz raw → 7.83 Hz with ionospheric cavity-height correction (Schumann 1952)
globe_value.m2_12p42: M2 = (lunar day)/2 = (1/(1/T_E − 1/T_M))/2 with T_E = 23.93419 h sidereal Earth rotation and T_M = 27.32158 d sidereal lunar orbit at 60 R_Earth → 12.4205 h (Doodson 1921 tabulation 12.4206)
globe_value.s2_12p00: S2 = 360°/(2 × 15.000°/h) = 12.000 h; 15°/h is the solar diurnal angular speed on a sphere rotating once per solar day
globe_value.k1_23p93: K1 = T_E sidereal = 23.93419 h (Earth's sidereal rotation period — referenced for cross-link to WIN-049)
globe_value.hubble_70: H₀ ≈ 70 km/s/Mpc derived from Type Ia SNe distance ladder + CMB acoustic-peak positions in spherical FLRW geometry

dome_value.pure_relabeling_examples: WIN-025 (eclipse magnetic depression — Sq current relabeled), WIN-002 (Schumann 7.83 Hz adopted, mechanism renamed 'aetheric cavity'), tidal WINs WIN-045/046/049 (M2/S2/K1 periods adopted, mechanism renamed 'gear-driven luminary')
dome_value.silent_substitution_examples: WIN-002 (dome cavity formula gives ~22 Hz, dome adopts 7.83), WIN-067 (dome geometry predicts ~90% gravity drop at rim, dome adopts 0.53%)
dome_value.independence_check: Pure-relabeling cases never derive a number from dome parameters. Silent-substitution cases derive a different number from dome parameters and then quietly publish the globe number. In neither case is the published value a dome-geometry output.

derivation_chain_dependency.schumann_chain: 7.83 Hz ← Schumann 1952 ← angular Laplacian on sphere ← R_Earth = 6,371 km
derivation_chain_dependency.m2_chain: 12.4206 h ← Doodson 1921 ← Laplace tidal equations on rotating sphere ← T_E sidereal (23.93 h) AND T_M sidereal (27.32 d at 60 R_Earth)
derivation_chain_dependency.s2_chain: 12.000 h ← 360°/(2 × ω_solar) ← ω_solar = 15°/h on rotating sphere ← spherical Earth + 24 h solar day
derivation_chain_dependency.hubble_chain: 70 km/s/Mpc ← FLRW metric ← spherical isotropic 3D space + GR field equations + integrated SNe and CMB observables
derivation_chain_dependency.common_pattern: Every relabeled value is a many-step theoretical derivation product, not a raw measurement. The dome's adoption of the value implicitly adopts the entire derivation chain.

fingerprint_1.match_precision: Exact in all five cases — the dome publishes the same digits as the globe-theory output, because the digits ARE the globe-theory output.
fingerprint_1.why_it_matters: The section's current argument leaves the defender room to say 'every theory agrees with the same numbers — that is just empirical consistency.' Closing this escape requires showing that the matched numbers are not raw observations but theoretical predictions of the globe framework. Once that is established, the dome is not 'agreeing with standard physics about an observation' — it is 'adopting standard physics' theoretical output and stamping it with a different name.' The relabeling pattern then becomes inheritance of the entire validation chain that globe physics has earned over decades. This strictly strengthens the section's 'no novel empirical content' claim: relabeling not only fails to generate new content, it actively borrows the validation that justifies the existing content.

defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim: 7.83 Hz is just an observed frequency in ELF measurements — anyone can measure it. The dome citing it is no more 'globe-dependent' than citing the boiling point of water at sea level.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter: The boiling point of water is a measurement constant — pour water, measure temperature, write the number. 7.83 Hz is not. ELF noise contains a continuous spectrum from 1 Hz to 100+ Hz; the *specific* identification of 7.83 Hz as a discrete cavity resonance (rather than just a broadband peak) requires a theoretical model to predict that exactly this frequency should be a mode. Schumann's 1952 paper did not measure 7.83 Hz; it predicted f₁ ≈ 10 Hz (with cavity-height correction down to ~7.8) BEFORE observation confirmed the prediction. The number's status as 'the Schumann fundamental' depends on the prediction. A flat-disc theory adopting 7.83 Hz does not adopt a measurement — it adopts a globe-derived prediction whose validation depends on globe geometry.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim_2: M2 = 12.42 h is just the lunar tidal period — the gear-driven luminary geometry can produce a 24.84 h lunar return cycle and the period falls out by division. There is no globe-geometry assumption.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter_2: The lunar day of 24.84 h is itself a globe-geometry quantity: it equals 1/(1/T_E − 1/T_M) where T_E = 23.93 h sidereal and T_M = 27.32 d sidereal. The dome must produce both T_E and T_M to reach 24.84 h non-circularly. T_E = 23.93 h (sidereal rotation, not solar) only has meaning relative to fixed stars in 3D space — under flat-Earth dome geometry there is no 'sidereal' frame distinct from the rotating local frame; the sky rotates by setup, not by Earth motion. T_M = 27.32 d sidereal requires the Moon to complete one orbit relative to fixed stars in 3D space; the dome's local moon at 2,534 km altitude does not orbit a central body in Keplerian fashion (no central gravity, no orbital mechanics) and has no mechanism to produce a 27.32-day sidereal period. The dome can stipulate 24.84 h as a kinematic input — but that is fitting, not deriving, and it imports the globe relationship between two sidereal periods that the dome cannot produce.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim_3: Even if the values were originally derived in the globe framework, agreement-with-observation is theory-neutral. The dome stating 7.83 Hz and being right about it is independent of how the globe got to 7.83 Hz first.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter_3: Theory-neutral agreement requires a derivation that does not pass through globe geometry. The dome offers no such derivation for 7.83 Hz — its own cavity formula gives ~22 Hz (silent-substitution case, see Section 2.1 and WIN-002 detail_evidence). For M2 the dome offers 'half a 24.84 h lunar circuit' which inherits T_lunar = 24.84 h as input, not output. For Hubble H₀ the dome offers no derivation at all. 'Agreement' that requires importing the globe value is not theory-neutral — it is theory-dependent on the imported framework.

fingerprint_1.suggested_text: One sharper layer on relabeling: the values being adopted are theory-internal predictions, not raw measurements. The Schumann fundamental 7.83 Hz is the n=1 eigenvalue of the angular Laplacian on a sphere with R = 6,371 km — Schumann's 1952 derivation gives f_n = (c/(2π R_E))·√(n(n+1)), with the √2 factor at n=1 specifically the spherical-harmonic eigenvalue (no flat-disc analog). M2 = 12.4206 h is half the lunar day, where the lunar day = 1/(1/T_E − 1/T_M) requires both Earth's sidereal rotation T_E = 23.93 h (a 3D sphere-vs-stars quantity) and the Moon's sidereal orbit T_M = 27.32 d at ~60 R_Earth (a Keplerian orbital quantity). S2 = 12.000 h follows from a sphere rotating once per solar day at 15°/h. K1 = 23.93 h (cited in WIN-049) is exactly T_E sidereal. The dome adopts each of these values without producing them from disc geometry — its own cavity formula gives ~22 Hz, its local moon at 2,534 km does not orbit anything Keplerian, and there is no flat-disc 'sidereal' frame distinct from the rotating sky. Relabeling therefore does not just rename the cause; it inherits the validation chain — every prior measurement that confirmed Schumann 1952, Doodson 1921, and Laplace tidal theory becomes a borrowed credential for the dome version. The defender's reply 'every theory must agree with observation' fails because the values being matched are not observations but predictions of the framework being denied.

fingerprint_2.name: The 'derives from dome parameters' count statistic in Section 2b.2 overstates dome independence — the few WINs that pass that test derive from dome parameters that are themselves globe constants in disguise
fingerprint_2.kernel_of_truth: The section's headline statistic — 'Only N of M reviewed WINs derive their predicted value from dome parameters' — is correct at the syntactic level: most WINs simply adopt a number with no derivation script. A defender granting that point can still retreat to: 'fine, but the N that DO derive from dome parameters represent genuine dome physics — those are the unification core, the rest is incomplete-but-not-fake work.' This treats 'derives from dome parameters' as a sufficient marker of dome independence.
fingerprint_2.but_here_is_why_it_fails: Audit the WINs that do appear to derive from dome parameters and trace the parameters themselves. The Tesla domain (WIN-001) computes f = VA/(2·DISC_R) where VA = 1.574·c (= (π/2)·c to 0.2%, the spherical waveguide phase velocity) and DISC_R = 20,015.0 km (= π·R_Earth to 4 ppm); algebraically this reduces to c/(4·R_Earth) — the spherical-waveguide formula with renamed variables (documented in SEC-2b.1 fingerprint-1, WIN-001, SEC-2.1). The Tesla 1.57c = (π/2)c phase velocity is a known waveguide effect REQUIRING the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity (WIN-062 detail_evidence). Sun altitude (5,733 km), moon altitude (2,534 km), firmament height (9,086 km) used in any WIN with explicit derivation are the outputs of fits to WGS84 globe data — find_curve.py and test_curve_stretching.py explicitly minimize 'distance to (90−lat)·111.1 km' where 111.1 km/deg = π·R_Earth/180° (documented in WIN-062 expanded analysis and SEC-2.1 fingerprint-2). The disc radius itself was selected to make azimuthal-equidistant projection match WGS84 distances — a globe-fit, not an independent prediction. So the 'derives from dome parameters' filter does not select for dome-independence; it selects for derivations that use globe constants under different variable names. The N count therefore overstates dome independence; the actual dome-independent count is closer to zero.

quantitative_dependency_map.DISC_R: 20,015 km = π × R_Earth (WGS84) to 4.3 parts per million; selected via find_curve.py/test_curve_stretching.py to maintain WGS84 map distances
quantitative_dependency_map.VA: 1.574c ≈ (π/2)c to 0.2%; adopted from Tesla 1905 patent, which derived it as (π R_Earth)/0.08484 s — explicitly a half-circumference round-trip on a sphere
quantitative_dependency_map.sun_alt_5733_km: Outputs of fitting to globe-WGS84 latitude–distance data (find_curve.py); each value is the optimum that reproduces globe geometry through AE projection, not an independent geometric input
quantitative_dependency_map.moon_alt_2534_km: Same — fitted to reproduce globe-derived distances and angular subtenses
quantitative_dependency_map.firmament_height_9086_km: Same — fitted, not derived from independent flat-disc physics
quantitative_dependency_map.consequence: Substituting the globe-equivalent expressions for any of these into a dome-derived formula reduces the formula to its globe original. The 'dome derivation' is a relabeling of variables, one level above the 'value relabeling' Section 2b.2 already documents.

fingerprint_2.why_it_matters: Section 2b.2's count statistic is the strongest sentence in the section because it converts the relabeling pattern into a prevalence claim. The fingerprint extends that conversion one level deeper: even within the small set of dome-derived WINs, the derivations themselves are globe formulas with renamed variables. The defender's last refuge — 'a few WINs at least represent genuine dome physics' — collapses into 'a few WINs are globe physics with one extra layer of relabeling.' This is methodologically powerful because it shows the relabeling pattern is not a peripheral feature of a few weak WINs but the structural mechanism of the entire model: at the value level (Section 2b.2 current text), at the formula level (this fingerprint), and at the parameter level (DISC_R = π·R_Earth, VA = (π/2)c).

defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim: Even if DISC_R was originally chosen for map-fit reasons, calling it 'globe-derived' is a chain-of-custody argument, not a logical one. The dome model takes DISC_R as a primitive parameter from this point forward; subsequent derivations are dome-internal.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter: Chain of custody matters when assessing predictive content. A model whose 'free parameters' are calibrated to reproduce another framework's geometry, then used to 'predict' quantities that depend on those parameters, is curve-fitting one framework to another and reporting agreement. The 4 ppm match of DISC_R to π·R_Earth is not 'a primitive parameter that happens to equal π·R_Earth' — it is the AE projection identity, mathematically required for the dome map to reproduce WGS84 distances. Once DISC_R is fixed by that identity, the 'dome derivation' VA/(2·DISC_R) is determined up to the choice of VA — and VA = (π/2)c is similarly fixed by the requirement to match Tesla's waveguide constant. There is no remaining free dome parameter contributing to the prediction; every degree of freedom has been spent matching the globe.

fingerprint_2.suggested_text: Extending the 'derives from dome parameters' statistic: the small handful of WINs that pass that filter derive from dome parameters that are themselves globe constants in disguise. The Tesla domain (WIN-001), which appears to compute a prediction from disc geometry, uses VA = 1.574·c (= (π/2)·c, the spherical-waveguide phase velocity) and DISC_R = 20,015 km (= π·R_Earth to 4 ppm); substitution reduces the formula VA/(2·DISC_R) to c/(4·R_Earth) — the globe spherical-waveguide fundamental with relabeled variables. Sun altitude (5,733 km), moon altitude (2,534 km), and firmament height (9,086 km) used in other WIN derivations are outputs of fitting scripts (find_curve.py, test_curve_stretching.py) that minimize distance to WGS84 latitude–distance data, where 111.1 km/deg = π·R_Earth/180°. The 'derives from dome parameters' filter therefore does not isolate dome-independent physics; it isolates globe formulas with renamed variables. Combined with the value-relabeling pattern this section already documents, the model exhibits relabeling at three levels: parameters (DISC_R = π·R_Earth), formulas (VA/(2·DISC_R) = c/(4·R_Earth)), and predicted values (Schumann, M2, S2, Hubble). What looks like an alternative geometry is the globe in costume.

suggested_patch.action: Append fingerprint_1 suggested_text after the 'Why this matters' paragraph (which currently ends with 'Relabeling N separate standard-physics results as aetheric does not constitute unification — it is re-attribution without new empirical content. The dome model's re-narration generates no such novel predictions.'). Then append fingerprint_2 suggested_text after that, as a deeper structural extension. The two paragraphs together convert the section from 'dome adopts standard-physics observations' to 'dome adopts standard-physics derivation chains AND derivations themselves use globe parameters in disguise', strictly strengthening the no-novel-content argument.
suggested_patch.tldr_impact: TLDR remains correct: it already says 'No new observable is predicted.' The fingerprints deepen the why without changing the thrust, so no TLDR change is required. If a TLDR sharpening is desired, append a half-clause: '…and the few WINs that do derive from dome parameters use parameters that are globe constants in disguise.'
suggested_patch.cross_reference_notes: Link fingerprint-1 paragraph to WIN-002 (silent substitution example) and WIN-045 (tidal tautology). Link fingerprint-2 paragraph to WIN-001 and SEC-2.1 (AE projection identity), and to SEC-2b.1 fingerprint-1 (Tesla VA/DISC_R reduction) since they are the same mechanism applied at the monitoring layer.

files_checked: monitor.py (verified in SEC-2b.1 fingerprint via raw fetch 2026-04-16)
find_curve.py and test_curve_stretching.py (referenced from WIN-062 detail_evidence and SEC-2.1 fingerprint-2)
code_search.found_in: DISC_R = 20015.0 and VA = 1.574*C are literal constants in monitor.py (no derivation). Sun/moon/firmament altitudes appear as fitted outputs of find_curve.py routines that minimize against WGS84 (90−lat)·111.1 km targets. No script derives any of these values from independent flat-disc physics.
code_search.notes: The 'derives from dome parameters' filter in Section 2b.2 picks up domains where a formula of the form f(constants) is evaluated. None of those formulas takes an input that is itself dome-derived; every input traces back to a globe constant or globe-fit output.
code_search.verification_method: Re-confirmed via prior SEC-2b.1 fingerprint code_search (2026-04-16) and WIN-062/SEC-2.1 cross-references. No new scripts examined for SEC-2b.2 — the section discusses an aggregate pattern, and the per-WIN code searches that establish the pattern have already been done in prior fingerprints.
